samples.xml_description: Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output product. Bit width for inputs A and B. Bit width for output product. Shift register to store the multiplicand A. Accumulator for the product. Counter to track the number of shifts. Flag to indicate completion of multiplication. // Reset the shift register and product register shift_reg <= A; product_reg <= 0; counter <= 0; done <= 0; // Check if multiplication is done if (counter < BIT_WIDTH && !done) begin // Check if the current bit of the multiplier is set if (B[counter] == 1'b1) begin // Add the shifted multiplicand to the product register product_reg <= product_reg + shift_reg; end // Shift the multiplicand left for the next addition shift_reg <= shift_reg << 1; // Increment the counter counter <= counter + 1; end else begin // Set the done flag to indicate completion of multiplication done <= 1; end // Assign the product register to the output port product_reg;
